peer_support_and_advice.description: Emotional support, personal advice, peer assistance, and help-seeking among community members

technical_support_and_troubleshooting.description: Technical help, bug reporting, troubleshooting, problem-solving for products or software

knowledge_and_information_sharing.description: Sharing facts, resources, educational content, and general information with the community

news_and_current_events.description: Aggregating, sharing, and discussing news, current events, and updates

discussion_and_debate.description: General discussion, debate, commentary, and exchange of opinions on any topic

content_aggregation_and_curation.description: Collecting, curating, and surfacing links, media, or content from elsewhere

fan_community_and_franchise.description: Fan communities organized around a specific franchise, IP, show, game, or media property

game_discussion_and_strategy.description: Gameplay discussion, strategy sharing, theorycrafting, build optimization, and game-specific advice

humor_and_memes.description: Meme creation and sharing, humor-focused content, jokes, and comedic culture

creative_expression_and_showcase.description: Sharing original creative work, projects, art, writing, photos, or other user-generated content

question_and_answer.description: Structured Q&A, recommendation-seeking, decision support, and direct question-answering formats

media_sharing_and_consumption.description: Sharing and passively consuming videos, clips, images, screenshots, and other media

skill_development_and_learning.description: Learning, skill-building, peer education, how-to guides, and educational resources

career_and_professional_guidance.description: Career advice, professional development, industry discussion, and workplace guidance

community_coordination_and_events.description: Organizing community events, coordination, scheduling, voting, and community logistics

consumer_and_product_discussion.description: Product reviews, recommendations, purchasing advice, and equipment or gear discussion

social_connection_and_community_building.description: Building relationships, cultural exchange, social bonding, and fostering community identity

civic_and_local_engagement.description: Civic participation, local community hubs, neighborhood discussion, and public affairs

entertainment_and_passive_consumption.description: Entertainment-focused communities primarily for passive enjoyment with low interaction expectations

other.description: Tags that do not clearly fit any of the above canonical categories

mixed_expertise_levels.description: Communities spanning a wide range of knowledge and skill depth, from beginners to experts, with no dominant tier

expertise_asymmetry.description: Significant imbalance between expert and novice members, creating potential for knowledge gatekeeping or mentorship dynamics

casual_to_hardcore_spectrum.description: Participants range from low-investment casual consumers to deeply committed enthusiasts or competitive players

broad_age_range.description: Community includes members across multiple generational cohorts, from young users to older adults

youth_presence.description: Meaningful proportion of minors or young demographics, elevating age-related moderation considerations

adult_focused.description: Community is primarily or explicitly oriented toward adult participants

vulnerable_populations.description: Members who may be at elevated risk due to financial, emotional, informational, legal, or developmental factors

misinformation_susceptibility.description: Participants are at heightened risk of encountering or spreading false or misleading information

global_audience.description: Membership is geographically distributed across multiple countries or regions, including multilingual and cross-cultural participants

general_audience.description: No specific demographic, expertise, or interest prerequisites; open to the broad public

low_barrier_to_entry.description: Community requires minimal prior knowledge, skill, or credentials to participate meaningfully

enthusiast_community.description: Participants share a strong common interest or fandom, ranging from hobbyists to dedicated superfans

professional_practitioners.description: Community includes or is centered on people with industry credentials, career expertise, or vocational stakes

competitive_players.description: Participants engaged in ranked, tournament, or performance-oriented play or competition

information_seekers.description: Members primarily present to find answers, learn, or receive advice rather than to contribute expertise

career_stage_diversity.description: Participants span multiple professional life stages including students, job seekers, career changers, and veterans

large_scale_community.description: High total membership volume, increasing anonymity, lurker ratios, and moderation complexity

parasocial_dynamics.description: Relationships between community members and public figures or creators that may affect member behavior and vulnerability

mentorship_dynamic.description: Structural tendency for more experienced members to guide or advise newer participants

technical_literacy_variance.description: Wide spread in members' ability to understand or engage with technical, digital, or domain-specific content

internet_native.description: Participants are culturally fluent in internet norms, memes, and platform behaviors

geographically_local.description: Community is anchored to a specific region, city, or locale rather than distributed globally

other.description: Tags that do not fit cleanly into any defined canonical category

misinformation_risk.description: Potential for false, misleading, or unverified information to spread and cause harm within the community

content_permanence.description: Risks arising from content being permanently indexed, searchable, or publicly visible long-term

harassment_risk.description: Potential for targeted harassment, bullying, doxxing, or personal attacks against individuals

chilling_effect.description: Risks that moderation practices, gatekeeping, or community norms suppress legitimate participation or discourse

hate_speech_risk.description: Potential for discriminatory, dehumanizing, or bigoted content targeting protected groups

financial_harm_risk.description: Potential for scams, fraud, exploitation, or bad financial advice causing real monetary loss

privacy_violation_risk.description: Potential for exposure of private personal information, including doxxing and unauthorized data sharing

spoiler_harm.description: Risk of unwanted exposure to plot or outcome spoilers that degrades the experience for community members

community_fragmentation.description: Risk of community splitting, polarization, echo chambers, or erosion of shared norms

spam_and_exploitation.description: Vulnerability to spam, commercial exploitation, malware, or low-quality content degrading the community

health_and_safety_misinformation.description: Potential for medically or physically dangerous misinformation that could cause direct harm to people

minor_protection_risk.description: Risk of exposing minors to inappropriate content, exploitation, or unsafe interactions

legal_and_liability_risk.description: Potential for content that creates legal exposure, including piracy, defamation, or regulated speech

content_quality_degradation.description: Risk of declining overall content quality through reposts, low-effort posts, or noise drowning out signal

psychological_harm_risk.description: Potential for content to cause psychological distress, trauma, or harm to vulnerable users

toxicity_risk.description: General risk of hostile, toxic, or unwelcoming community atmosphere that drives away members

radicalization_and_normalization_risk.description: Risk of content normalizing extremist views, hate, or gradually shifting community norms toward harmful ideologies

technical_harm_risk.description: Potential for bad technical advice or malicious content causing hardware damage, data loss, or security breaches

brigading_risk.description: Risk of coordinated off-platform or cross-community attacks manipulating or overwhelming the community

trust_erosion.description: Risk of declining trust between community members, moderators, or between community and platform

censorship_tension.description: Tension between free expression and content removal, where over- or under-moderation risks perceived censorship

newcomer_discouragement.description: Risk that community norms, gatekeeping, or hostility deter new members from participating

other.description: Stakes-related risks that do not fit cleanly into any other canonical category

low_conflict.description: Communities with minimal interpersonal conflict, whether by norm, enforcement, or culture (low_conflict, minimal_conflict, low_hostility, low_antagonism, conflict_averse, low_interpersonal_conflict, conflict_minimized, low_conflict_norm, low_conflict_enforced, low_conflict_moderated, low_conflict_expectation, low_conflict_norms, low_conflict_encouraged, low_conflict_enforcement, low_conflict_baseline, conflict_managed, low_to_moderate_conflict)

civility_enforced.description: Communities where respectful behavior is actively moderated or explicitly required (civility_enforced, civil_enforcement, civil_enforced, low_toxicity_enforced, rule_enforced, rule_conscious, respectful_required, respectful_enforcement, moderated_civility)

informal_casual.description: Communities with relaxed, everyday conversational register (informal, informal_casual, informal_conversational, casual_informal, casual_friendly, casual_conversational, casual_respectful, informal_friendly, highly_informal, low_formality, conversational, conversational_informal, supportive_informal)

semi_formal.description: Communities balancing accessibility with some degree of structure or professionalism (semi_formal, moderate_formality, moderately_formal, mixed_formality, professional_accessible, accessible_language)

formal.description: Communities with a high degree of formality, professional tone, or academic register (formal_professional, formal_analytical, formal_academic, formal_register, formal_respectful, respectful_formal, professional_formal, professional_pragmatic, professional_helpful, respectful_formality)

humor_heavy.description: Communities where humor, memes, or comedy are central to the culture (humor_present, humor_heavy, humor_centric, humor_focused, humor_driven, meme_heavy, meme_culture, meme_friendly, humorous, irreverent, playful_irreverent, playful_tone, casual_irreverent, irreverent_humor, absurdist_humor, entertainment_focused)

low_humor.description: Communities where humor is present but peripheral or restrained (low_humor, minimal_humor, light_humor, gentle_humor, low_casual_humor, low_to_moderate_humor, low_humor_tolerance, moderate_humor)

dark_or_edgy_humor.description: Communities featuring dark, self-deprecating, dry, or self-aware comedic styles (dark_humor, dark_humor_present, self_deprecating_humor, self_deprecating, dry_humor, self_aware_humor, self_aware_irony)

collaborative_helpful.description: Communities oriented around mutual aid, problem-solving, and cooperative participation (collaborative, helpful_collaborative, collaborative_helpful, supportive_collaborative, collaborative_spirit, helpful_pragmatic, pragmatic_helpful, professional_helpful, helpful_supportive, helpful_tone, solution_oriented, solution_focused)

supportive_encouraging.description: Communities emphasizing emotional support, encouragement, and positive reinforcement (supportive, encouraging, supportive_encouraging, inclusive_welcoming, inclusive, welcoming, friendly_welcoming, non_judgmental, beginner_friendly)

evidence_analytical.description: Communities that prioritize facts, evidence, data, or rigorous analysis (evidence_focused, evidence_based, fact_focused, formal_analytical, detail_oriented, substantive_focus, technical_focused, quality_focused)

debate_oriented.description: Communities that engage in structured argumentation, debate, or contested discussion (debate_oriented, constructive_debate, respectful_debate, passionate_debate, respectful_disagreement, constructive_feedback)

technical.description: Communities using specialized or technical language, often with some accessibility accommodation (informal_technical, casual_technical, technical_accessible, technical_pragmatic, technical_but_accessible)

respectful.description: Communities with a broadly respectful tone not captured by formal enforcement (respectful, respectful_discourse, civil_discourse, civil_respectful, respectful_civil, respectful_baseline, respectful_boundaries)

enthusiastic_passionate.description: Communities characterized by high energy, excitement, or strong emotional investment in a topic (enthusiastic, enthusiastic_positive, enthusiastic_casual, passionate_fandom, passion_driven, celebratory, emotionally_expressive)

pragmatic_direct.description: Communities favoring straightforward, practical, no-nonsense communication (pragmatic, direct_communication, practical_focused, practical_direct, technical_pragmatic)

conflict_present.description: Communities where some level of tension, frustration, or conflict is a normal or accepted feature (occasional_conflict, frustration_present, occasional_frustration, competitive_intensity, competitive_banter, low_to_moderate_conflict)

community_vernacular.description: Communities with highly specific in-group language, slang, or cultural norms (gaming_vernacular, profanity_normalized)

other.description: Tags that do not fit cleanly into any other canonical category
